Bhimaratha is the modern Bhuna, a tributary^ of the^Kfsna. It
is also called Bhaimarathi.
Mahanadi rises in the Amarakantaka range, flows through
Onssa and falls into the Bay of Bengal.
NirvindhyZ is identified with the Nevuz, a tributary of the
Chambal between the Betwa and the Kali-Sindh in Malwa. Kali-
dasa refers to it in his Meghaduta (I. 28-9).
Vetravati is the modern Betwa, a tributary of the Yamuna.
Sipra is a tributary of the Chambal into which it falls a little
below Sitamau. The city of Ujjain is situated on its bank.
&ona is the modern Sone which falls into the Gahga near
Patna. Megasthencs calls it Erannaboas (Hiranyavaha) at whose
